name: Lee Konitz
abstract: Leon "Lee" Konitz (October 13, 1927 – April 15, 2020) was an American jazz alto saxophonist and composer. He performed successfully in a wide range of jazz styles, including bebop, cool jazz, and avant-garde jazz.

interviews.title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: I made a million records with the guy. So we – we just did a duo – we have a duo record come out in September. We have a record with Lee Konitz coming out in March.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Javon Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: I forgot to mention Lee Konitz, a person I’m very fond of and a mentor as well.
about_quotes.doc_title: Javon Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 3, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part I
about_quotes.evidence: The band was — well you know how they have the Monday night bands here like when Thad Jones and Mel Lewis used to play at the Vanguard every Monday night. about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part I interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Schuller Gunther
about_quotes.evidence: It was of course the members of the Modern Jazz Quartet. It was Dave Brubeck. It was Dizzy Gillespie. And then some of the younger ones. It was Jimmy Giuffre. about_quotes.doc_title: Schuller Gunther — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Danko
about_quotes.evidence: I also met Lee Konitz and started playing with him. ... I lived on the upper west side. But he lived right across the street from Striker’s where I was playing a couple of nights with Chet Baker and then I started playing a couple of nights with Lee.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Danko interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 28, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Elvin Jones
about_quotes.evidence: so I made an appointment with Lee Konitz it was just a trio: Lee, Sonny Dallas and myself at a studio... We just started playing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Elvin Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
